description_long: This object is a compact, modern spaceplane with a streamlined white fuselage and sharply swept wings. The nose is rounded and black, featuring a cockpit with multiple angular windows, and the body displays United States insignia and flags. The rear section houses two prominent gold-colored engine nozzles and a central circular exhaust, with a vertical stabilizer and two upward-canted winglets. The wings are mid-mounted and sharply swept, with black undersides and leading edges. The surface is smooth and glossy, with visible panel lines and hatches suggesting access points and functional compartments. The overall design is reminiscent of advanced orbital or suborbital vehicles, optimized for both atmospheric and spaceflight operations.
description: A sleek, white spaceplane with swept mid-mounted wings, a black nose, and gold engine nozzles, featuring a forward cockpit and United States markings.
description_view_0: Rear view showing twin gold engine nozzles, central exhaust, and upward-canted winglets.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the sharply swept wing, vertical stabilizer, cockpit windows, and United States insignia.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the rounded black nose, angular cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with visible engine nozzles, wing, and national markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and upward-canted winglets; no visible dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin rear-mounted jet engines with gold-colored nozzles and a central circular exhaust.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two upward-canted horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main and nose gear doors visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple angular windows, likely single or dual-seat.
